name: mlua
description: High-level Lua bindings for Rust supporting Lua 5.1-5.5, LuaJIT, and Luau

implementation.comment: Rust bindings to Lua C library; vendored compilation available

typing.comment: Lua is dynamically typed; Luau adds optional type annotations

paradigm.comment: Lua supports imperative, functional, and prototype-based OOP via metatables

closures.comment: Lua functions are closures with proper lexical scoping

pattern-matching.comment: Lua lacks native pattern matching; relies on if/elseif chains

error-handling.comment: Lua uses pcall/error for exceptions; mlua wraps these in Rust Result types

async-await.comment: No native async/await in Lua scripts; mlua provides async via Rust integration

iterators-generators.comment: Generic for loop with iterator functions; coroutines can act as generators

standard-library.comment: Standard libs: string, table, math, io, os, debug, coroutine, utf8

execution-strategy.comment: Standard Lua uses bytecode VM; LuaJIT option provides JIT compilation

compilation-model.comment: Can load source at runtime or precompile to bytecode

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be reloaded via standard Lua mechanisms

repl-support.comment: Standard Lua includes REPL; mlua can evaluate strings interactively

debugging-support.comment: Hooks, stack inspection, tracebacks via debug library and mlua APIs

source-maps.comment: Line number tracking and tracebacks available

startup-time.comment: Standard Lua has minimal startup; LuaJIT slightly higher but still fast

execution-speed.comment: Standard Lua is fast bytecode; LuaJIT achieves near-native with JIT

memory-overhead.comment: Lua is designed for embedding with small footprint (~200KB core)

garbage-collection.comment: Lua 5.4 supports incremental and generational GC modes

pure-rust.comment: Bindings to Lua C library (vendored or system)

api-safety.comment: All Lua API errors protected by lua_pcall; unsafe contained internally but library admits 'huge amount of unsafe code'

idiomatic-api.comment: Excellent Rust patterns: traits (IntoLua, FromLua, UserData), Result types, async support, derive macros

async-compatible.comment: Async feature with create_async_function, AsyncThread; works with any executor

function-registration.comment: UserData derive macros, UserDataMethods builder API, closure registration

type-mapping.comment: IntoLua/FromLua traits with implementations for standard types; serde for complex types

no-std-compatible.comment: Requires std for Lua C integration and memory management

wasm-compatible.comment: Supports wasm32-unknown-emscripten target; non-JIT Lua versions only

blank-slate-mode.comment: Can create Lua state without standard libraries via Lua::new_with() specifying StdLib

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory

cpu-limits.comment: set_hook with every_nth_instruction for execution limits; set_interrupt for Luau

syscall-restrictions.comment: Can omit io/os libraries via StdLib flags; Luau sandbox mode restricts further

capability-security.comment: StdLib flags control which libraries are available; Luau sandbox mode

default-deny.comment: By default includes standard libraries; must explicitly restrict via Lua::new_with()

share-data.comment: UserData for references, BorrowedStr/BorrowedBytes for zero-copy, regular conversion clones

operator-overloading.comment: MetaMethod enum for __add, __sub, __index, etc. via UserDataMetatable

method-syntax.comment: UserDataMethods registers methods callable with colon syntax (obj:method())

callbacks-events.comment: Functions can be stored and called back from Rust

crates-io-downloads.comment: Estimated based on ~150k monthly downloads; lib.rs shows 38k weekly

package-manager.comment: Can create modules for LuaRocks ecosystem

editor-support.comment: Lua has mature tooling: lua-language-server LSP, VS Code extensions, formatters

example-projects.comment: 286 reverse dependencies; used in game engines, Neovim plugins, web frameworks

documentation-quality.comment: Good API docs with examples; no dedicated book but comprehensive docs.rs

maturity.comment: First released 2019, now at v0.11.5 with widespread production use

primary-use-cases.comment: Lua's traditional strengths: game scripting, config, extensibility
